rules: [P1] If elapsed time exceeds the protocol interval, create the appropriate vaccine order; otherwise emit a concise no‑action FINISH.
[P1] Retrieve Immunization records for the target vaccine code before deciding.
[P1] Select the most recent administration date and compute days elapsed to today.

[P1] When aggregating Observation values, filter by the required time‑window, ensure unit consistency, compute the specified aggregation (e.g., mean), and include the number of observations and the overall time range; if none remain, explicitly state absence.

[P1] When evaluating existing orders of a given type, filter by both the medication (or order code) and the exact clinical indication; if none match, create the required order; if exactly one matches, emit a concise no‑action statement; if multiple match, keep the newest and discontinue the older ones.
[P1] Ensure the action string matches the expected schema (method, URL, parameters only).
[P1] Always compute days elapsed from the latest relevant record (or none) to today before deciding.

[P1] Query required FHIR resources (Observation, MedicationRequest, Procedure, ImagingStudy, DeviceUseStatement, Immunization, etc.) before any decision; retrieve the most recent instance, cite its value/timestamp (or explicitly state none), and treat missing data as unmet condition.
[P1] Compare retrieved numeric values against protocol‑defined normal ranges; if within range, emit a concise no‑action statement, otherwise follow the appropriate decision path.
[P1] If a required observation is missing, explicitly state its absence and, when the protocol permits, make a decision based on the available data.
[P1] Emit exactly one action line per turn; for FINISH actions include a single string and no extra prose.
[P1] After any creation or discontinuation, explicitly confirm the step taken (e.g., "Created order X", "Discontinued order Y").
[P1] Convert month‑or year‑based intervals to days before comparing elapsed time.
[P1] Never create orders based on assumptions; always validate all prerequisite data.
[P1] When creating an order, use the protocol‑recommended medication, dosage, route, and frequency, and cite the creation with identifiers and timestamps.

[P1] When checking vaccination status, retrieve Immunization records for the relevant vaccine code, select the most recent administration date, compute days elapsed to today, and if it exceeds the recommended interval, create the vaccine order; otherwise emit a concise no‑action FINISH.
[P1] When multiple resources of the same type are needed, request them in a single API call using a comma‑separated code list or a superset time window, then filter locally.
[P1] When retrieving Procedure resources, filter by the relevant procedure code(s), select the most recent performed date, and use it for any time‑based threshold calculations.

[P1] For device duration checks, retrieve the DeviceUseStatement placement timestamp, compute elapsed days to the current date, and if it exceeds the protocol threshold and no removal order exists, create the appropriate service request; otherwise emit a concise no‑action FINISH statement.
[P1] If no prior instance exists or the latest instance is older than the specified interval, create the order with the exact clinical indication; otherwise emit a concise no‑action FINISH.
[P1] Do not create or cancel orders outside the defined category.
[P1] When ordering, include the exact clinical indication provided in the instruction.

[P1] For dependent orders, verify prerequisite orders exist; if none exist emit a concise no‑action statement; otherwise ensure matching dependent orders are present, creating any missing per protocol while avoiding duplicates.
[P1] Before concluding a medication class is absent, explicitly query the patient's active medication list for that class.
[P1] If no prior immunization record exists for the required vaccine, treat it as overdue and create the order.
